column2: Assumption
Users find it motivating, not deflating, to see a personal-best comparison on a day they lost money.
Users want the evening Post-Game send in addition to Pre-Match, not perceiving it as spam or redundant.
The system can reliably compute a meaningful personal-best discipline streak from historical data.
The system can reliably detect end-of-trading-day and send Post-Game at the right local time.
Users who had a bad discipline day still want the Post-Game recap rather than muting it.
The briefing contains enough information to feel valuable, not just a superficial gamified wrapper.
Users prefer the basketball-framed Pre-Match briefing over glancing at their broker app directly.
Users care about their live exposure/margin even on days they don't plan to trade.
The risk-to-stat mapping engine can be built and stays accurate across a wide range of account states.
Enough beta signups (80%) successfully connect an account so Pre-Match has data to work with.
The AI generates fresh, accurate narration for both Pre-Match and Post-Game daily, without factual drift or repeating itself between the two sends.
Combined AI generation cost across both Pre-Match and Post-Game stays sustainable per user per month, before any monetization layer exists.
Neither the daily exposure framing (Pre-Match) nor the loss-reframed-as-a-win framing (Post-Game) nudges users toward taking on more real trading risk than they otherwise would.
Users are willing to open both daily touchpoints, the morning Pre-Match and the evening Post-Game, not just one of them.
Adding Post-Game Recap on top of Pre-Match measurably improves week 4 retention or daily open rate enough to justify Post-Game's additional build and running cost.
The two required cTrader MCP calls return complete, accurate data every morning, not just once.
Users can read and understand the Pre-Match briefing quickly, within a few seconds, without it feeling like a chore.
Personal-best comparison drives interest in the Post-Game recap, beyond the recap's content alone.
New users understand the basketball vocabulary used across both Pre-Match and Post-Game, shot clock, foul, box score, personal best, without needing a tutorial.
The Post-Game recap contains the important information so users can act on it.
Users can tell from the notification preview alone whether tonight's Post-Game news is good or bad.
The personal-best mechanic stays meaningful over time and doesn't become discouraging once a record is hard to beat.
The fixed local-time morning send reliably lands before the user starts their day.
Users can tell at a glance whether today looks safe or risky without reading the full Pre-Match narration.
